title: foundation — permissions
description: Platform-admin-only permissions gating foundation's diagnostic surface (module registry, health detail, search index management, config reload). No sanctum-guard permissions — foundation exposes no tenant-facing surface beyond public ping/health, which is unauthenticated. Seeded by `FoundationPermissionSeeder` with `is_system=true`.

permissions.name: platform.modules.view
permissions.description: Read the module registry: `GET /api/v1/platform/modules` + `GET /api/v1/platform/modules/{module}`. Reveals which modules loaded + their dependency graph. Restricted because it discloses deployment structure.

permissions.name: platform.health.view
permissions.description: Read detailed health probe output (`GET /api/v1/platform/health/detail`) + subscribe to the `platform.health` broadcast channel. Detailed output includes error messages which may leak internals — restricted to trusted ops staff.

permissions.name: platform.search.manage
permissions.description: Reindex or flush a Scout search index (`POST /api/v1/platform/search/reindex`, `POST /api/v1/platform/search/flush`). Reindex is safe (async job dispatch); flush is destructive + requires confirmation.

permissions.name: platform.config.reload
permissions.description: Trigger a config hot-reload (`POST /api/v1/platform/config/reload`). Sensitive — an incorrect config in prod after a Doppler push can be surfaced across every module without a redeploy.

notes: Public health endpoints (`GET /api/ping`, `GET /api/health`, `GET /up`) are UNAUTHENTICATED and thus not permission-gated. They intentionally leak the minimum information required for a load balancer probe.
There are no sanctum-guard permissions here. Foundation is invisible to tenant users.
